trend: Mild bullish for the next 1-3 trading days while NIFTY holds 24200-24090; upside confirmation still requires a sustained move above 24300-24380 because 24300 remains the immediate Call OI wall and FII index futures remain net short.
entry: Direction: buy dips only; entry zone 24200-24260 while above 24090; expected probability about 62-64%; risk-reward about 1:1.8 toward 24500 and 1:2.5 toward 24800.
stoploss: Below 24090 on decisive intraday breakdown or closing basis
bullish_reasons: NIFTY spot is positive around 24269-24280 and remains above option-chain max pain of 24250.
DII cash flow remains positive at +1784 Cr as of 2026-07-02, absorbing latest FII cash selling.
India VIX is lower near 11.80-11.88, supporting lower-volatility conditions.
Latest option-chain PCR is about 0.974, inside the 0.9-1.2 bullish band.
Price is above max pain 24250 and holding above the 24200-24090 tactical support band.
USDINR is stable-to-softer intraday near 95.29, which is supportive versus a sharp dollar rise.
Brent crude remains under pressure and is heading for a fourth weekly decline, helped by Hormuz traffic normalization and US-Iran talks.
Global cues are broadly supportive: Asian markets closed green and latest US futures cues on the GIFT Nifty page are positive.
Domestic/news backdrop remains constructive: IT buying, cooling crude, softer Fed-hike concerns, and market commentary favoring buy-on-dips while 24200 holds.
bearish_reasons: FII cash flow is still negative at about -312 Cr as of 2026-07-02.
FII index futures positioning remains net short at about -256900 contracts, with cash and futures aligned bearish.
Bank Nifty is mildly negative near 57929 and is underperforming NIFTY.
Highest Call OI / immediate wall remains around 24300, just above spot, limiting upside until a breakout sustains.
Change-OI PCR near 0.475 and heavier 24300 Call OI indicate fresh call-side pressure despite bullish spot action.
GIFT Nifty is -0.08% with a small implied discount of about -8 points, so it is not a fresh bullish trigger.
